Document:
# Non-security is only permitted to hunt # station-wide or round-ending threats
Validhunting is defined as hunting antagonists that pose no active threat to you or your department, in particular as a non-security member. It is also commonly referred to as vigilantism.
Spending a majority of a round to hunt a specific "valid" target is dissallowed unless they provide reason through escalation.
## Examples of Validhunting
- A clown is witnessed with an item that can only be bought from a traitor's uplink. A Scientist sees this and chases the clown through maintanence tunnels and most of the station before killing them with a toolbox. - A captain hears about a crew member that broke into a department and stole something. Despite the presence of an active security team, the captain forgoes their duties and begins working by himself to hunt this crew member. - Medical staff has been whispering to eachother slightly, the captain breaks in to force a department search on reason that "they were acting like they were doing something they shouldn't."
## Examples of accepted Validhunting
- A Syndicate operative is running around in a blood-red hardsuit holding a c-20, a botanist takes this time to go to the armory and grab a weapon to fight along with the rest of security. - A revolution has been proven to have started and has been deemed to have taken over the majority of non-mindshielded crewmembers. The Head of Personnel with some other mindshielded crew tries to search out unmindshielded people to restrain and deconvert.
Note, the Security departments job is to deal with station threats and is not bound by this rule.
